meta.description: BloodHound-shaped ACL findings for Meridian Financial. Each entry represents a misconfigured ACE that creates an attack path.

acl_misconfigurations.attack_description: svc-deploy can modify any attribute on the IT-Admins group, including its membership (member attribute). An attacker who controls svc-deploy can add any account (including jsmith) to IT-Admins.
exploit_steps: Obtain svc-deploy credential (Kerberoast TGS in module 03, or password reuse)
Use ldapmodify or impacket to add target user to IT-Admins group membership
Target user now has IT-Admin rights on all servers where IT-Admins is local admin
acl_misconfigurations.remediation: Remove the GenericWrite ACE for svc-deploy on IT-Admins. Audit all service accounts for write rights on security groups.

acl_misconfigurations.attack_description: Any member of Finance-Managers (currently amurphy) can add members to Finance-Users. This allows lateral movement to any Finance OU resource accessible to Finance-Users.
exploit_steps: Access Finance file share and payroll application
acl_misconfigurations.remediation: Restrict Finance-Managers to 'Allow Add/Remove Members' only via fine-grained ACE, not GenericWrite. Or use a helpdesk workflow instead of delegated group management.

acl_misconfigurations.attack_description: Helpdesk-Staff members (rrodriguez, qwalker) can reset passwords for all Finance OU users without knowing the current password. Compromise of a helpdesk account = immediate access to all Finance accounts.
exploit_steps: Compromise rrodriguez or qwalker credential (both have weak password Welcome1!)
Reset jsmith or clee (Payroll-Access) password to attacker-controlled value
Log in as Finance user, access payroll or finance shares
acl_misconfigurations.remediation: Restrict password reset delegation to specific accounts only (not all Finance OU users). Require approval workflow for privileged account password resets. Enforce strong passwords for Helpdesk accounts.

acl_misconfigurations.attack_description: svc-backup has unconstrained delegation. Any user who authenticates to the backup service will have their TGT stored in memory on the backup server. If the DC's machine account is coerced to authenticate (e.g., via printer spooler abuse), the DC TGT is retrievable and can be used to forge any ticket.
exploit_steps: Obtain shell/access on the server running svc-backup (via Kerberoast in module 03)
Monitor LSASS on that server with Rubeus / mimikatz for incoming TGTs
Coerce DC machine account to authenticate (printerbug/petitpotam)
Extract DC$ TGT from memory, use it for DCSync or golden ticket
acl_misconfigurations.remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation from svc-backup. Convert to constrained delegation limited to BackupSvc/backup01.meridian.local only. Or migrate to a gMSA.

attack_paths.name: jsmith to Domain Admins via ACL chain
